steps.title: Spot the need for a modifier
steps.content: After the blank the sentence already contains another verb. This means the blank cannot be a finite verb; it must supply a non‑finite modifier such as a participle to describe a cultural studies analysis.

steps.title: Apply the participial modifier
steps.content: Choosing “focusing” gives the phrase “a cultural studies analysis focusing on the intersection … might yield,” where “focusing” is a present participle modifying the noun. The other choices would create a sentence with two finite verbs lacking a proper conjunction, breaking the rule that a clause needs only one finite verb unless joined correctly. That matches choice C — C is correct.
